pg77-p1-s2: At the moment we are trying to put our lives in order.
pg77-p1-s3: But this is not an end in itself.
pg77-p1-s4:
Our
real purpose is to fit ourselves to be of maximum service to God and the people about us.
pg77-p1-s5:
It is seldom wise to approach
an individual, who still smarts from our injustice to him, and announce that we have gone religious.
pg77-p1-s6:
In the prize ring,
this would be called leading with the chin.
pg77-p1-s7: Why lay ourselves open to being branded fanatics or religious bores?
pg77-p1-s8:
We may
kill a future opportunity to carry a beneficial message.
pg77-p1-s9:
But our man is sure to be impressed with a sincere desire to
set right the wrong.
pg77-p1-s10:
He is going to be more interested in a demonstration of good will than in our talk of spiritual
discoveries.
pg77-p1:
We might prejudice them. At the moment we are trying to put our lives in order. But this is not an end in itself. Our
real purpose is to fit ourselves to be of maximum service to God and the people about us. It is seldom wise to approach
an individual, who still smarts from our injustice to him, and announce that we have gone religious. In the prize ring,
this would be called leading with the chin. Why lay ourselves open to being branded fanatics or religious bores? We may
kill a future opportunity to carry a beneficial message. But our man is sure to be impressed with a sincere desire to
set right the wrong. He is going to be more interested in a demonstration of good will than in our talk of spiritual
discoveries.
pg77-p2-s1: We don't use this as an excuse for shying away from the subject of God.
pg77-p2-s2:
When it will serve any good purpose, we are
willing to announce our convictions with tact and common sense.
pg77-p2-s3:
The question of how to approach the man we hated will
arise.
pg77-p2-s4:
It may be he has done us more harm than we have done him and, though we may have acquired a better attitude
toward him, we are still not too keen about admitting our faults.
pg77-p2-s5:
Nevertheless, with a person we dislike, we take the
bit in our teeth.
pg77-p2-s6: It is harder to go to an enemy than to a friend, but we find it much more beneficial to us.
pg77-p2-s7:
We go to
him in a helpful and forgiving spirit, confessing our former ill feeling and expressing our regret.
pg77-p2:
We don't use this as an excuse for shying away from the subject of God. When it will serve any good purpose, we are
willing to announce our convictions with tact and common sense. The question of how to approach the man we hated will
arise. It may be he has done us more harm than we have done him and, though we may have acquired a better attitude
toward him, we are still not too keen about admitting our faults. Nevertheless, with a person we dislike, we take the
bit in our teeth. It is harder to go to an enemy than to a friend, but we find it much more beneficial to us. We go to
him in a helpful and forgiving spirit, confessing our former ill feeling and expressing our regret.
pg77-p3-s1: Under no condition do we criticize such a person or argue.
pg77-p3-s2:
Simply we tell him that we will never get over drinking until
we have done our utmost to straighten out the past.
pg77-p3-s3:
We are there to sweep off our side of the street, realizing that
nothing worth while
pg77-p3:
Under no condition do we criticize such a person or argue. Simply we tell him that we will never get over drinking until
we have done our utmost to straighten out the past. We are there to sweep off our side of the street, realizing that
